data_source_summary: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (Chhattisgarh); NFSM Portal (nfsm.gov.in); Official Notification (NFSM, April 2007)

Q1.src: NFSM Portal (nfsm.gov.in); Official Notification dated April 1, 2007

Q2.v: Agriculture Department, Government of Chhattisgarh (State Implementing Agency); Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Government of India (Central Nodal Ministry)

Q3.v: NFSM National Guidelines (2007 notification); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (Chhattisgarh); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; NFSM Portal (nfsm.gov.in); Chhattisgarh Agriculture Portal (agriportal.cg.nic.in/nfsm)

Q4.r: The primary resource on which NFSM operates is private agricultural land held individually by beneficiary farmers. The scheme's core activities — improved seed distribution, input subsidy for pulse and oilseed production, and demonstration plots on individual holdings — are all directed at enhancing productivity on privately cultivated farmland. No common land, community water body, or shared natural resource is the primary operating resource. The scheme is structured as a private-good input subsidy (seed + inputs to individual farmers for production on their own fields). Demonstration plots and extension training are ancillary and do not constitute the primary resource.
Q4.src: NFSM National Guidelines (2007); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 (budget lines: Pulse production support ₹45 cr, Oilseed enhancement ₹35 cr, Seed distribution ₹5 cr — all directed to individual private farmers); Economic Survey 2024-25

Q5.v: Private agricultural land across 26 districts (pulses: toor, urad, moong) and 12 districts (oilseeds: groundnut, mustard, soybean) in Chhattisgarh. Individual farmers cultivate these crops on their own private holdings. Scheme activities: improved HYV seed supply, input subsidy, farmer-level demonstration plots, and extension training. Beneficiaries: 1.2 lakh farmers (pulses) and 85,000 farmers (oilseeds). No commons boundary or common pool resource is the primary resource; each beneficiary's field is privately owned or held.
Q5.src: Economic Survey 2024-25 (Chhattisgarh); NFSM Portal; CAG Report 2024-25

Q6.r: The primary resource (private agricultural farmland of individual beneficiaries) has no commons boundary demarcation. Individual farm plots are identified in revenue records but there is no public document defining a commons resource boundary. T1.5 = 0: FLAG — T4.2 measurement risk noted.
Q6.src: Revenue land records (general); NFSM scheme guidelines do not define any common resource boundary

Q7.r: The primary resource is private land. Community access or practice rights (statutory, customary, or otherwise) are not applicable to individually owned private farmland. Each farmer holds individual ownership or tenancy rights over their own land — not a commons access right. T1.2 = 0 → T1 FAIL condition triggered.
Q7.src: NFSM Guidelines; Land records principle (private holdings)

Q8.r: No active diversion order, mining lease, change-of-land-use notification, SEZ notification, or compulsory acquisition proceeding applies to NFSM's primary operational resource (private farmland) during the scheme period. T1.6 = No → PASS condition met.
Q8.src: No encumbrance notification found in scheme documents, CAG report, or official portal records

Q9.breakdown: Pulse production support: ₹45 Cr; Oilseed enhancement: ₹35 Cr; Training and extension: ₹10 Cr; Seed distribution: ₹5 Cr
Q9.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (fund utilization: 94% of ₹95 Cr allocation verified)

Q10.r: Training and extension activities (₹10 Cr) represent expenditure on a public/common good — knowledge dissemination, demonstrations with non-excludable learning spillovers, and capacity building accessible to all participating farmers and potentially to non-beneficiary farmers in the area. This is the only expenditure component classifiable as non-private under T1.3 definitions. Demonstration plots (sub-component of extension) create non-excludable demonstration effects. All other components (pulse production support ₹45 Cr, oilseed enhancement ₹35 Cr, seed distribution ₹5 Cr) flow exclusively as subsidies or inputs to individual private farmers for production on their private lands.

Q11.r: Pulse production support (₹45 Cr) + Oilseed enhancement (₹35 Cr) + Seed distribution (₹5 Cr) = ₹85 Cr. These are input subsidies (seeds, fertilizers, micro-nutrients) and direct seed provision flowing to individual private farmers for production on their own private farmland. Outputs accrue exclusively to the individual beneficiary household. Q10 + Q11 = ₹95 Cr = Q9 (verified).
Q11.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 (budget line breakdown); NFSM Guidelines (component-wise activities)

Q12.interp: FAIL — θ₁ = 0.1053 is well below the 0.50 threshold. Only 10.5% of scheme expenditure is directed at non-private/common resources (training and extension). The remaining 89.5% is input subsidy and seed distribution to individual private farmers for private land cultivation.
Q12.src: Derived from Q9 and Q10 values; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26

docs: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 (NFSM allocation and component-wise breakdown)
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — Chhattisgarh (NFSM fund utilization audit)
Outcome Budget 2025-26 (NFSM outcomes and expenditure results)

Q14.r: Benefits of NFSM are fully excludable. Improved seeds, input subsidies, and the resulting crop income accrue exclusively to individual private farmer beneficiaries on their own private plots. Non-beneficiaries cannot access the scheme's primary outputs (seeds, subsidies, crop income). Extension/training benefits have some positive externality but are not the primary economic output of the scheme. T1.4 = 0 → T1 FAIL condition triggered.
Q14.src: NFSM Guidelines (individual beneficiary targeting); Economic Survey 2024-25 (beneficiary lists); NFSM Portal (beneficiary database)

pass_verification.C1_T1.1: FAIL — T1.1 = C (private land); automatic T1 FAIL triggered
pass_verification.C2_T1.2: FAIL — T1.2 = 0; no community access or practice right applicable to private agricultural land
pass_verification.C4_T1.4: FAIL — T1.4 = 0; benefits fully excludable to individual private farmers
pass_verification.C5_T1.6: PASS — T1.6 = No; no active legal encumbrance on primary resource
